seoTitle: How to Edit an Issue in Jira
metaDescription: Learn how to update the fields on a Jira issue, from the summary and description to assignee, priority, and other details.
intro: Issues rarely stay static once created, and knowing how to update them properly keeps your project data accurate. This guide covers the basics of editing an existing Jira issue.

steps.heading: Open the issue
steps.body: Navigate to the specific issue you need to update.

steps.heading: Edit the relevant field
steps.body: Click into the field you want to change, whether that's the summary, description, assignee, priority, or another attribute.

steps.heading: Make your update
steps.body: Enter the new information, replacing what was there before or adding to existing content as needed.

steps.heading: Save the change
steps.body: Most fields save automatically or with a quick confirmation click once you've made your edit.

steps.heading: Review the issue's history
steps.body: Check the activity log if you want to confirm what was changed and when, especially on issues with multiple contributors.

faq.question: Can anyone edit a Jira issue, or does it require special permission?
faq.answer: Editing typically requires edit permissions on the project, which may be limited depending on your role and the project's permission scheme.

faq.question: Is there a record of changes made to an issue?
faq.answer: Yes, Jira keeps an activity log on each issue showing the history of field changes and comments.

faq.question: Can I edit multiple issues at once?
faq.answer: Yes, Jira supports bulk editing for certain fields, letting you update several issues simultaneously instead of one at a time.

faq.question: What happens if two people edit the same issue at the same time?
faq.answer: Jira generally handles this by saving the most recent change, so it's good practice to refresh the issue before editing if you know others are also working on it.
